annotations.label: Cantor's theorem

anchor.snippet: is a fundamental result which states that

annotations.note: `Cardinal.cantor` states `a < 2 ^ a` for every cardinal, i.e. a set has strictly smaller cardinality than its power set.

annotations.label: Cantor's theorem for finite sets

anchor.snippet: can be seen to be true by simple enumeration

annotations.note: The finite case `n < 2^n` is a direct instance of the general `Cardinal.cantor`, which holds for all (including finite) cardinals.

annotations.label: Cardinality of the reals exceeds the integers

anchor.snippet: which is the same as that of the power set

annotations.note: `Cardinal.mk_real : #ℝ = 𝔠 = 2^ℵ₀` gives reals = power set of ℕ, and `Cardinal.aleph0_lt_continuum` shows this exceeds ℵ₀ = #ℤ.

annotations.label: No largest cardinal number

anchor.snippet: there is no largest cardinal number

annotations.note: The `NoMaxOrder Cardinal` instance (Order.lean:344) is derived directly from `Cardinal.cantor`, formalizing that no largest cardinal exists.

annotations.label: Cardinality comparison

anchor.snippet: By definition of cardinality, we have

annotations.note: `Cardinal.le_def : #α ≤ #β ↔ Nonempty (α ↪ β)` is the definitional characterization of cardinal comparison via injections.

annotations.label: No surjection onto power set

anchor.snippet: there is no surjective function from any set

annotations.note: `Function.cantor_surjective` states that no `f : α → Set α` is surjective, exactly the no-surjection-onto-power-set claim.

annotations.label: Cantor diagonal set

anchor.snippet: sometimes called the Cantor diagonal set of

annotations.note: The diagonal set `{a | a ∉ f a}` appears inline within the proof of `Function.cantor_surjective` but is not a standalone named definition.

annotations.label: Theorem (Cantor)
annotations.note: The boxed statement |A| < |𝒫(A)| is `Cardinal.cantor` (with the surjection form available as `Function.cantor_surjective`).

annotations.label: Cantor's theorem for countably infinite A

anchor.snippet: Let us examine the proof for the specific case

annotations.note: `Cardinal.aleph0_lt_continuum : ℵ₀ < 𝔠 = 2^ℵ₀` is exactly Cantor's theorem for the countably infinite set ℕ.

annotations.label: Cantor's paradox

anchor.snippet: is the name given to a contradiction following from

annotations.note: The underlying fact (no largest cardinal, via the `NoMaxOrder Cardinal` instance from `Cardinal.cantor`) is formalized, but the paradox of a set of all cardinals is not stated.

annotations.label: Russell set

anchor.snippet: into what is sometimes called the Russell set

annotations.note: Mathlib's type theory has no unrestricted comprehension, and grep found no formalization of the Russell set `{x | x ∉ x}`.

annotations.label: Russell's paradox

anchor.snippet: is straightforwardly adapted to show that

annotations.note: Russell's paradox does not arise in Lean's type theory and is not formalized in Mathlib (the closest analog being `Function.not_surjective_Type`).

annotations.label: Cantor's 1891 indicator-function form

anchor.snippet: is a function defined on X whose values are 2-valued functions

annotations.note: `Function.cantor_surjective` over `Set α` (= `α → Prop`, the 2-valued functions on α) is exactly Cantor's 1891 indicator-function formulation.

annotations.label: More propositional functions than objects

anchor.snippet: there are more propositional functions than objects

annotations.note: Propositional functions on α are `α → Prop ≃ Set α`, so `#α < #(Set α)` (`Function.cantor_surjective`) is the claim that there are more predicates than objects.

annotations.label: Zermelo's Cantor's Theorem
annotations.note: Zermelo's statement that every set has lesser cardinality than its power set is precisely `Cardinal.cantor`.

annotations.label: Lawvere's fixed-point theorem

anchor.snippet: provides for a broad generalization of Cantor's theorem

annotations.note: `Function.exists_fixed_point_of_surjective` is the Type-category instance of Lawvere's fixed-point theorem, as its docstring explicitly notes.
